data.title: PBMs reportedly eyeing changes to business conduct to avert regulatory scrutiny
data.description:
Pharmacy benefit managers, which function as intermediaries between pharmacies and health insurers, are working on a proposal to voluntarily adjust some of their business operations in a bid to avoid fresh regulatory scrutiny from the Trump administration, Bloomberg News reported.

The PBM trade group Pharmaceutical Care Management Association has prepared draft proposals to bring to the attention of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services ahead of potential new regulations against the industry, according to a document seen by Bloomberg News.

PCMA includes leading PBM operators such as CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), Cigna (NYSE:CI), UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV), and Humana (HUM).

Among proposals cited in the report are plans to widen the availability of low-cost alternatives to biologics and increase payments to rural and independent pharmacies. A proposal to ensure that insured patients will not pay more than a pharmacy would charge someone without insurance is also under discussion.

To convince the CMS, PCMA is forwarding a range of ideas, including an argument to indicate that a well-publicized initiative will trigger favorable media coverage, according to the document.

The initiative comes as successive U.S. governments increasingly target PBMs for their role in driving up drug prices in the U.S. President Donald Trump issued an executive order in May calling on pharma companies to lower U.S. drug prices and pledging he will also “cut out” pharma middlemen from the sector.

data.title: Ticketmaster agrees to UK watchdog's terms after Oasis reunion tour complaints
data.description:
Live Nation-owned Ticketmaster (LVY) on Thursday agreed to commitments listed out by the UK's Competition and Markets Authority about the way it sells passes and advertises them after concertgoers who attended the Oasis' reunion tour complained about high ticket prices.

The regulator opened an investigation into Ticketmaster UK Ltd. and found the company did not tell fans waiting in lengthy queues that standing tickets were being sold at two different prices and that prices would jump as soon as the lower-priced tickets sold out.

CMA also found that Ticketmaster did not provide sufficient explanation to customers and sold some "platinum" tickets at almost two and a half times the price of "standard" tickets.

Concertgoers had complained the company used an algorithmic pricing model, or "dynamic pricing," to sell Oasis tickets, but the CMA's investigation did not find any evidence of such sales tactics.

Going forward, the UK watchdog has ordered the company to notify customers 24 hours in advance if a tiered pricing system is being used for an event, provide more information about ticket prices during online queues, not use any misleading ticket labels, and provide regular reports showing how it has complied with the commitments.

"We welcome the CMA’s confirmation there was no dynamic pricing, no unfair practices, and that we did not breach consumer law," Ticketmaster said.

data.title: Wall Street drops as jobless claims fall and Q2 GDP was revised higher
data.description:
Wall Street’s recent downward push continued on Thursday as the broader market averages observe selling pressure after jobless claims fell and GDP was revised higher in the third estimate.

Falling the hardest has been the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) which has dropped 0.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has found itself lower by 0.5% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) is in the red by 0.3%. 

Across sectors, 10 of the 11 S&P groups are in negative trading territory, with Health Care suffering the most. At the other end, Energy has been the strongest performing segment on the session so far. 

U.S. Treasury yields are also on the move higher. The longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) pushed up 4 basis points to 4.19% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 5 basis points to 3.66%. At the same time the dollar index (DXY) tagged a 3-week high.

On the economic front, initial jobless claims dropped for the second week.

Q2 GDP was revised higher to +3.8% in the third estimate, intensifying the rebound.

Durable goods orders surprised higher in August, while the U.S. international goods trade deficit narrowed more than expected in August.

“Thursday's upward GDP revision for second quarter confirmed that the economy grew at a healthy clip, even as tariff uncertainty reached fever pitch during the quarter. The U.S. economy is resilient and the strong GDP is another indication that we are not at risk of any kind of recession, even with slowing labor market growth,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated.

“Thursday's GDP strength likely doesn't change the Federal Reserve's expected path of rate cuts, since the data is backward looking and was already well-known. The Fed wants to lower rates to keep the labor market in check and is able to do so at this time since inflation has remained under control, even though it's still above the Fed's target,” Stanley added.

Furthermore, the Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day.

In terms of Fed speak, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, New York Fed President John Williams, Fed Governor Michael Barr and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, among others, are all slated to take the stage in different events.

data.title: Palo Alto Networks gets HSR early termination for CyberArk acquisition
data.description:
Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) has received an early termination notice for its planned $25 billion acquisition of CyberArk (NASDAQ:CYBR). 

The early termination notice was disclosed on the Federal Trade Commission's website on Thursday. 

Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) agreed to acquire identity security firm CyberArk (NASDAQ:CYBR) for an equity value of about $25 billion in late July.

data.title: Centrus Energy launches multibillion dollar expansion of Ohio uranium plant
data.description:
Centrus Energy (NYSE:LEU) +9.9% in Thursday's trading after detailing plans for a multibillion dollar expansion of its uranium enrichment facility in Piketon, Ohio, boosting production of low enriched uranium and high assay low enriched uranium production.

Subject to being selected for funding by the U.S. Department of Energy, Centrus (NYSE:LEU) said its expansion plans call for a multibillion dollar public and private investment to add thousands of additional centrifuges at the American Centrifuge Plant, delivering large-scale production of both LEU and HALEU.

The project also is expected to support hundreds of jobs at Centrus' (LEU) centrifuge manufacturing plant in Tennessee and throughout its domestic manufacturing supply chain, the company said.

In anticipation of the expansion, Centrus (LEU) said it has raised more than $1.2 billion in a pair of convertible note transactions, has secured more than $2 billion in contingent purchase commitments from utility customers, and recently announced a collaboration with Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power and POSCO International for a potential investment in the project.

data.title: NORAD jets intercept Russian aircraft near Alaska
data.description:
The North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) said Thursday it scrambled fighter jets a day earlier to track four Russian military planes operating near Alaska.

According to the command, two Tu-95 bombers and two Su-35 fighters were detected in the Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone on Wednesday. In response, NORAD dispatched an E-3 airborne warning plane, four F-16 fighters, and four KC-135 refueling aircraft.

The Russian planes stayed in international airspace and did not cross into U.S. or Canadian territory, NORAD said. The Alaskan identification zone, which covers international airspace, requires all aircraft to be readily identifiable for security reasons.

data.title: TD SYNNEX projects $23B–$24B Q4 billings as AI and cloud demand accelerate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TD SYNNEX Corporation (SNX) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Patrick Zammit, CEO, highlighted, "our third quarter non-GAAP gross billings and diluted earnings per share established new records for our company" and cited "consolidated gross billings were $22.7 billion, growing 12%, 10% in constant currency. And non-GAAP diluted earnings per share of $3.58 exceeded the high end of our guidance, representing a 25% increase year-over-year." Zammit pointed to strong performance from Hyve, noting "gross billings increasing in the mid-30s year-over-year and ODM/CM gross billings increasing 57% year-over-year, fueled by continued strength in hyperscaler investments in cloud infrastructure." He also cited a 26% increase in software gross billings and "healthy momentum across each of our regions with exceptionally dynamic performance in Latin America and Asia Pacific and Japan." Zammit announced the launch of TD SYNNEX Partner First in North America to unify partner experience and confirmed expansion of the Destination AI enablement program, focusing on "Agentic AI, security for AI and AI factory."
  • Marshall Witt, CFO, stated, "we achieved 12% gross billings growth and 25% non-GAAP diluted EPS growth in the third quarter, which exceeded the high end of our guidance range." Witt also noted, "net revenue was $15.7 billion, up 7% year-over-year and above the high end of our guidance range. Gross profit increased 18% year-over-year to $1.1 billion." He explained, "Free cash flow was $214 million, driven by strong earnings growth and a slight improvement in our cash conversion cycle." Witt confirmed a dividend of $0.44 per share payable October 31, 2025, and total year-to-date return to stockholders of $534 million.

Outlook

  • Marshall Witt announced Q4 guidance: "For the fourth quarter, we expect gross billings in the range of $23 billion to $24 billion, representing an increase of approximately 11% at the midpoint." He added, "Net revenue in the range of $16.5 billion to $17.3 billion," and "non-GAAP net income in the range of $281 million to $322 million. Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share in the range of $3.45 to $3.95 per diluted share." Witt anticipates a non-GAAP effective tax rate of approximately 23% and interest expense of $91 million.
  • Zammit stated, "we are confident...for next quarter" regarding Hyve customer demand, and expects similar strength in Q4, with supply chain service demand also supporting momentum.

Financial Results

  • Witt reported, "Endpoint Solutions portfolio increased gross billings 10% year-over-year, driven by continued demand for PCs... Advanced Solutions portfolio increased gross billings by 13% year-over-year and 8% year-over-year when excluding the impact of Hyve." He detailed, "Gross margin as a percentage of gross billings was 5%, which increased 23 basis points year-over-year and improved sequentially." Non-GAAP SG&A expense was $655 million or 3% of gross billings, with a cost to gross profit percentage of 58% in Q3, improved from 60% in the first half of the year. Non-GAAP operating income was $475 million, and operating margin as a percentage of gross billings was 2.09%.
  • The company ended the quarter with $874 million in cash and cash equivalents and debt of $4.2 billion. Gross leverage ratio was 2.3x and net leverage ratio 1.8x. Gross cash days were approximately 16 days, a 1-day improvement sequentially.

Q&A

  • Maya, Morgan Stanley, asked about Hyve dynamics for Q4 and next year. Zammit responded, "the quarter did -- I mean, went extremely well as did distribution, by the way. So what explains the overperformance? First, I mean, we saw growth, significant growth across all the programs and all the customers." He added confidence for Q4. Witt added, "we continue to make investments in skill sets, engineering capabilities, capacity, manufacturing, et cetera, to ensure that we stay ahead of capacity requirements."
  • David Paige Papadogonas, RBC, asked about PC pull forward and free cash flow targets. Zammit indicated, "we think it's very limited...we continue to see very good momentum on PCs across the world." Witt stated, "our expectation is that the free cash flow will be approximately $800 million for the year," adjusting from the $1.1 billion previously targeted, citing increased working capital needs.
  • Keith Housum, Northcoast Research, asked about sustainability and pull forward from Q4 into Q3. Zammit indicated the demand drivers would "continue into Q4."
  • Michael Ng, Goldman Sachs, asked about Hyve's new customer onboarding and growth sources. Witt replied, "we continue to make good progress in what we'll call programs... Our pipeline remains quite healthy and strong." Zammit added, "the growth is coming from networking and compute and more traditional compute."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on sustainability, free cash flow, and Hyve growth, with a slightly positive to neutral tone, seeking clarification on pull-forward dynamics and future guidance.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, especially on Hyve and PC segments, using phrases like "we are confident" and "we believe that, I mean, those dynamics will remain in Q4." Witt was direct in addressing the adjusted free cash flow guidance, reflecting transparency.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more assertive regarding growth momentum, while analysts remained cautiously optimistic but pressed for sustainability details.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from caution around growth rates and pull-forward effects in Q2 to greater confidence in Q3, with higher gross billings and EPS targets for Q4.
  • Strategic focus evolved from executing digital and service initiatives in Q2 to launching new platforms such as Partner First and expanding AI enablement in Q3.
  • Analyst focus moved from macro risks and demand volatility to the sustainability of high growth and cash flow conversion.
  • Key metrics like gross billings, EPS, and gross margin all saw year-over-year and sequential improvements, with free cash flow guidance revised lower reflecting higher working capital needs.
  • Management conveyed increasing confidence in core growth drivers, particularly Hyve and AI-related demand, in contrast to more cautious language and macro uncertainty in Q2.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged that "Hyve is a lumpy business" and results can vary by quarter, but expressed confidence in current customer demand and continued investments in capacity and skills.
  • Zammit highlighted that federal sector remains a small but strategic part of the portfolio, facing "anticipated softness...as our customers navigate a dynamic environment."
  • Witt cited increased working capital requirements as impacting free cash flow, particularly due to Hyve's longer cash conversion cycle.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of growth, potential for pull-forward, and adjusted cash flow guidance, with management providing clarity and rationale for changes.

Final Takeaway

TD SYNNEX delivered record gross billings and EPS in Q3 2025, driven by robust demand in cloud, AI, and PC markets, as well as standout performance from Hyve and software segments. Management projects continued momentum into Q4 with gross billings guidance of $23 billion to $24 billion and reinforced confidence in ongoing growth drivers, even as free cash flow expectations were revised lower due to elevated working capital needs. New digital initiatives and AI-focused programs are expected to further strengthen the company’s competitive position and support long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S., South Korea in talks to fine-tune $350B trade agreement - report
data.description:
The U.S. is looking to fine-tune its trade agreement with South Korea, according to a media report on Thursday.

South Korea's President Lee Jae Myung said Tuesday that the negotiations with the U.S. over tariffs had caused instability in the country's foreign exchange market; however, he showed confidence that a solution will soon be reached.

U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick was in recent talks with South Korean officials discussing the idea of slightly boosting the $350B it had committed to the U.S. in July, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter, including an advisor to the South Korean government.

On the financing side, the Trump administration wants more of the South Korea funding to be given in cash rather than loans, some of the people added.

The U.S. wants to revise its deal with South Korea, though the White House has avoided seeking changes that would significantly diverge from the original terms, a White House official told the WSJ, adding that the complete framework has yet to be made public.

South Korea's trade agreement is largely modeled on Japan's, the article said, and -- together with the U.K.'s -- stands among the limited number of deals that both nations have officially signed.

ETFs to track the South Korean market: (NYSEARCA:EWY) (NYSEARCA:KORU) (NYSEARCA:FLKR).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Worthington Steel outlines AI-driven cost savings and market share gains amid ongoing automotive momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Worthington Steel (WS) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Geoffrey Gilmore welcomed the newly acquired Sitem team, stating, “We completed our acquisition of 52% of Sitem in June. To our Sitem teammates who may be on the call, we are thrilled to have you join the Worthington family, and I'm excited about what we'll accomplish together.” Gilmore highlighted the company’s “disciplined execution in a soft market, resulting in year-over-year volume growth,” and reported adjusted EBITDA of $75.2 million, earnings per share of $0.72, and net sales of $872.9 million.

  • Gilmore emphasized, “An important highlight of our quarter was safety. Through training, continuous improvement and the commitment of every Worthington Steel employee, we achieved our safest quarter on record.” He described ongoing cautious optimism, noting, “Visibility is limited in several sectors, and we expect this to persist for the near term. That said, we are cautiously optimistic despite continued uncertainty in the market.”

  • The company saw “continued growth in automotive with new programs ramping up to drive volume,” with Detroit 3 production up 5% year-over-year and Worthington Steel’s shipments increasing by nearly 13%. Gilmore added, “We also offset some of the slowness in the heavy truck market with an increase in market share during our first quarter.”

  • Strategic updates included progress on electrical steel investments, with production in Canada scheduled for early calendar year 2026 and a Mexico facility set to begin production in a few months. The Sitem acquisition expands the company’s reach in the global EV market.

  • Gilmore discussed the integration of artificial intelligence, stating, “This quarter, we identified, launched and are advancing 4 critical AI-driven pilots: demand forecasting, predictive inventory optimization, predictive maintenance and forecast and demand planning automation. All 4 of these are expected to provide cost savings and/or free up cash flow when fully implemented.”

  • CFO Timothy Adams stated, “For the first quarter, we are reporting earnings of $36.8 million or $0.72 per share as compared with earnings of $28.4 million or $0.56 per share in the prior year quarter.” Adams detailed the Sitem acquisition’s financial structure and described unique items impacting results, including a $0.01 negative EPS impact from currency exchange and a $4.6 million onetime bonus paid to Sitem employees.

Outlook

  • Management expects limited visibility in several sectors to persist, maintaining a “cautiously optimistic” outlook for the near term. Gilmore indicated optimism in automotive, stating, “We project we'll probably finish the year at like 15 million unit build rate, which honestly, we're pleased with.”

  • Adams noted, “Given that many of our contracts use lagging index-based pricing mechanisms, we expect to generate inventory holding losses in the second quarter of fiscal 2026. We estimate those losses could be approximately $5 million to $10 million as compared with the $5.6 million of estimated holding gains in the current quarter.”

Financial Results

  • Net sales were $872.9 million and adjusted EBIT was $54.9 million, up $15.5 million from the prior year quarter. Gross margin increased by $14.8 million year-over-year, primarily due to higher direct material spreads and higher direct volumes, offset by lower toll processing gross margin.

  • Direct sales volume made up 63% of the mix for the quarter, up from 56% in the prior year quarter, with direct sale volume increasing 6%. Shipments to the automotive market rose 17% compared to the prior year, while construction and ag volumes declined. Toll processing volumes were down 22% year-over-year.

  • Cash flow from operations was a $5 million outflow and free cash flow was a $34 million outflow, with cash flows impacted by increases in working capital. CapEx for the quarter was $29 million, with a fiscal 2026 forecast of $100 million. The company ended the quarter with $78 million of cash and net debt of $155 million.

Q&A

  • Philip Gibbs, KeyBanc Capital Markets, asked for details on the Sitem acquisition’s mezzanine financing. Adams explained, “The Sitem purchase price was composed of $60 million in cash... combined with the contribution of the German facility. We financed the Sitem acquisition using the ABL.”

  • Gibbs inquired about future automotive share gains. Gilmore responded, “Regardless of the direction of the overall automotive market, yes, we've -- the commercial group has continued to do an excellent job gaining market share... The group continues to find opportunities. I think you'll continue to see some of that market share layered in, and we'll be coming up on contract season soon and I think we have some very good prospects there.”

  • Gibbs asked about Section 232 tariffs on electrical steel laminations. Gilmore stated, “We've seen little impact. We don't think we're going to see any material impact going forward. The customers are paying or are willing to pay the tariffs.”

  • John Tumazos, John Tumazos Very Independent Research, asked about any disruption from the U.S. Steel coke accident. Gilmore answered, “It's not going to have any impact on our business. Certainly, we have a great relationship with U.S. Steel, but we have equally good relationships with several other mill sources.”

  • Martin Englert, Seaport Research Partners, asked about toll volume declines and direct sale mix. Adams replied, “Half of that reduction is due to market conditions... the vast majority of the other piece of that is related to the Worthington Samuel Coil Processing shutdown.” Englert asked about future mix; Adams said, “I think our direct sale volume is probably going to be in that 60% to 65% range and toll will then be 35% to 40%.”

  • Englert asked about Q2 volumes and seasonal factors. Adams indicated, “Q1 is typically the average quarter and Q2 is usually 3% or 4% below that, and Q3 is usually 3% or 4% below Q1 as well. I think we would expect normal seasonality.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was largely neutral to slightly positive, focusing on capital allocation, market share, and risk mitigation. Questions about Sitem financing and automotive market share were answered with detail and transparency.

  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently referencing confidence in strategy and execution. Gilmore stated, “We are focused on what we can control and we are positioning ourselves to win in any environment.”

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the sentiment remains cautious but slightly more confident regarding automotive market share and AI-driven efficiencies, with ongoing vigilance around market uncertainty and input costs.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language continues to stress “cautious optimism,” with management reiterating limited visibility in several sectors. Strategic focus remains on automotive market share gains and electrical steel expansion.

  • The current quarter saw adjusted EBITDA of $75.2 million and EPS of $0.72, compared to $87 million adjusted EBITDA and $1.10 EPS in the previous quarter, reflecting lower inventory holding gains and higher SG&A from Sitem integration.

  • Management’s tone is consistently disciplined and focused on execution, with no significant shift in sentiment. Analysts continue to press for clarity on acquisition mechanics, volume outlook, and pricing dynamics, mirroring prior quarter concerns.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing uncertainty in the macro environment, visibility limitations, and expected inventory holding losses in the next quarter.

  • Adams highlighted exposure to market pricing for hot-rolled coil, stating, “Current pricing for hot-rolled coil is approximately $800 per ton, again, reflecting softer market demand.”

  • The ag market continues to experience challenges, with volumes down nearly 50% year-over-year.

  • Lower toll processing volumes were attributed to market softness and facility consolidation, with an expected annual decrease of approximately 100,000 tons.

  • Integration of Sitem introduces foreign exchange risk due to the euro-denominated minority interest and related EPS adjustments.

Final Takeaway

Worthington Steel began fiscal 2026 with strong execution, highlighted by integration of Sitem, gains in automotive market share, and measurable progress in AI-driven operational efficiencies. Management remains cautiously optimistic, focusing on cost discipline, strategic investments in electrical steel, and adaptability to ongoing market uncertainty. The company is positioning for continued growth and improved profitability, while remaining vigilant about risks in pricing, volumes, and integration challenges.
